revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]]" to "<nowiki>[[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]</nowiki>"

revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Twinkle standard installation}} ==When to use== Use this tag to identify '''personal essays'''. Personal essays describe the author's own emotional [[feeling]]s about a topic. Although Wikipedia is supposed to compile human knowledge, it is [[WP:What Wikipedia is not#PUBLISHER|not a vehicle to publish users' personal opinions]]. This template should be used when the article contains the editor's own personal, emotional comments on the subject. Use it when the article does not necessarily represent a blatant opinion or opinion piece, but is still overly judgmental in tone. ===When not to use=== '''Do not use this template to tag fact-oriented pages that sound like they might have been written as research papers for school''' (called "essays" in some parts of the world). Instead, if those pages need a different writing style or tone, use {{tl|Research paper}}, or general templates like {{tl|Tone}} or {{tl|Copy edit}}. If the article is biased, but without expressing the editor's emotions or value-based judgments, use tags like {{tl|unbalanced}} or {{tl|POV}}. ===How to use=== Place this: {{Template link with parameters|Essay-like|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} at the top of the article to alert editors to the inappropriate presence of editors' personal reflections throughout the article. ;Sections *To mark specific sections instead of the whole article, place {{tlx|Essay-like|section}} at the top of the section. ;Options *To replace the text "a personal reflection or essay", you may use {{tlx|Essay-like|article or section|yourtext}} or {{tlx|Essay-like|2=yourtext}} *Articles tagged without the optional date parameter will be given the parameter by a [[WP:Bots|bot]]. *Add a new item to the talk page explaining the problem so editors will know what to address, and when to remove this tag. ===Notes=== *This template will add tagged articles to dated subcategories of [[:Category:Wikipedia articles with style issues by month]] and to [[:Category:All articles with style issues]]. *This [[WP:Templates|template]] is a [[WP:Manual of Style/Self-references to avoid|self-reference]]. *Please do not [[WP:Substitution|subst:]] this template. ==See also== * [[Template:Editorializing]] – inline version of this template, for flagging particular statements * [[Template:Essay]] – identifies internal [[Wikipedia:Essays]] * [[Template:How-to]] * [[Template:Research paper]] * [[Wikipedia:Cleanup process]] * [[Wikipedia:Template messages/Cleanup]] ==TemplateData== {{TemplateData header}} { "description": "Use this cleanup template to indicate that an article is written like a personal reflection, personal essay, or argumentative essay that states a Wikipedia editor's personal feelings or presents an original argument about a topic.", "format": "inline", "params": { "date": { "label": "Month and year", "description": "The month and year that the template was placed (in full). \"May 2025\" inserts the current month and year automatically.", "type": "line", "autovalue": "May 2025", "example": "January 2013", "suggested": true }, "2": { "label": "Custom text", "description": "Text to replace \"a personal reflection, personal essay, or argumentative essay\" in the template", "type": "string", "example": "a forum post" }, "1": { "label": "Affected area", "description": "Text to replace the word \"article\", usually \"section\"", "type": "line", "autovalue": "section", "example": "section" }, "small": { "label": "Make template small", "description": "Enter \"left\" here to make the template box small and aligned to the left.", "type": "line", "autovalue": "left", "example": "left" } }, "paramOrder": [ "date", "2", "1", "small" ] } {{sandbox other|| [[Category:Tone cleanup templates]]}}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local/ILL link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Twinkle standard installation}} ==When to use== Use this tag to identify '''personal essays'''. Personal essays describe the author's own emotional [[feeling]]s about a topic. Although Wikipedia is supposed to compile human knowledge, it is [[WP:What Wikipedia is not#PUBLISHER|not a vehicle to publish users' personal opinions]]. This template should be used when the article contains the editor's own personal, emotional comments on the subject. Use it when the article does not necessarily represent a blatant opinion or opinion piece, but is still overly judgmental in tone. ===When not to use=== '''Do not use this template to tag fact-oriented pages that sound like they might have been written as research papers for school''' (called "essays" in some parts of the world). Instead, if those pages need a different writing style or tone, use {{tl|Research paper}}, or general templates like {{tl|Tone}} or {{tl|Copy edit}}. If the article is biased, but without expressing the editor's emotions or value-based judgments, use tags like {{tl|unbalanced}} or {{tl|POV}}. ===How to use=== Place this: {{Template link with parameters|Essay-like|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} at the top of the article to alert editors to the inappropriate presence of editors' personal reflections throughout the article. ;Sections *To mark specific sections instead of the whole article, place {{tlx|Essay-like|section}} at the top of the section. ;Options *To replace the text "a personal reflection or essay", you may use {{tlx|Essay-like|article or section|yourtext}} or {{tlx|Essay-like|2=yourtext}} *Articles tagged without the optional date parameter will be given the parameter by a [[WP:Bots|bot]]. *Add a new item to the talk page explaining the problem so editors will know what to address, and when to remove this tag. ===Notes=== *This template will add tagged articles to dated subcategories of [[:Category:Wikipedia articles with style issues by month]] and to [[:Category:All articles with style issues]]. *This [[WP:Templates|template]] is a [[WP:Manual of Style/Self-references to avoid|self-reference]]. *Please do not [[WP:Substitution|subst:]] this template. ==See also== * [[:en:Template:Editorializing|Template:Editorializing]] – inline version of this template, for flagging particular statements * [[Template:Essay]] – identifies internal [[Wikipedia:Essays]] * [[Template:How-to]] * [[Template:Research paper]] * [[Wikipedia:Cleanup process]] * [[Wikipedia:Template messages/Cleanup]] ==TemplateData== {{TemplateData header}} { "description": "Use this cleanup template to indicate that an article is written like a personal reflection, personal essay, or argumentative essay that states a Wikipedia editor's personal feelings or presents an original argument about a topic.", "format": "inline", "params": { "date": { "label": "Month and year", "description": "The month and year that the template was placed (in full). \"May 2025\" inserts the current month and year automatically.", "type": "line", "autovalue": "May 2025", "example": "January 2013", "suggested": true }, "2": { "label": "Custom text", "description": "Text to replace \"a personal reflection, personal essay, or argumentative essay\" in the template", "type": "string", "example": "a forum post" }, "1": { "label": "Affected area", "description": "Text to replace the word \"article\", usually \"section\"", "type": "line", "autovalue": "section", "example": "section" }, "small": { "label": "Make template small", "description": "Enter \"left\" here to make the template box small and aligned to the left.", "type": "line", "autovalue": "left", "example": "left" } }, "paramOrder": [ "date", "2", "1", "small" ] } {{sandbox other|| [[Category:Tone cleanup templates]]}}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local/ILL link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Twinkle standard installation}} ==When to use== Use this tag to identify '''personal essays'''. Personal essays describe the author's own emotional [[feeling]]s about a topic. Although Wikipedia is supposed to compile human knowledge, it is [[WP:What Wikipedia is not#PUBLISHER|not a vehicle to publish users' personal opinions]]. This template should be used when the article contains the editor's own personal, emotional comments on the subject. Use it when the article does not necessarily represent a blatant opinion or opinion piece, but is still overly judgmental in tone. ===When not to use=== '''Do not use this template to tag fact-oriented pages that sound like they might have been written as research papers for school''' (called "essays" in some parts of the world). Instead, if those pages need a different writing style or tone, use {{tl|Research paper}}, or general templates like {{tl|Tone}} or {{tl|Copy edit}}. If the article is biased, but without expressing the editor's emotions or value-based judgments, use tags like {{tl|unbalanced}} or {{tl|POV}}. ===How to use=== Place this: {{Template link with parameters|Essay-like|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} at the top of the article to alert editors to the inappropriate presence of editors' personal reflections throughout the article. ;Sections *To mark specific sections instead of the whole article, place {{tlx|Essay-like|section}} at the top of the section. ;Options *To replace the text "a personal reflection or essay", you may use {{tlx|Essay-like|article or section|yourtext}} or {{tlx|Essay-like|2=yourtext}} *Articles tagged without the optional date parameter will be given the parameter by a [[WP:Bots|bot]]. *Add a new item to the talk page explaining the problem so editors will know what to address, and when to remove this tag. ===Notes=== *This template will add tagged articles to dated subcategories of [[:Category:Wikipedia articles with style issues by month]] and to [[:Category:All articles with style issues]]. *This [[WP:Templates|template]] is a [[WP:Manual of Style/Self-references to avoid|self-reference]]. *Please do not [[WP:Substitution|subst:]] this template. ==See also== * [[:en:Template:Editorializing|Template:Editorializing]] – inline version of this template, for flagging particular statements * [[Template:Essay]] – identifies internal [[Wikipedia:Essays]] * [[:en:Template:How-to|Template:How-to]] * [[Template:Research paper]] * [[Wikipedia:Cleanup process]] * [[Wikipedia:Template messages/Cleanup]] ==TemplateData== {{TemplateData header}} { "description": "Use this cleanup template to indicate that an article is written like a personal reflection, personal essay, or argumentative essay that states a Wikipedia editor's personal feelings or presents an original argument about a topic.", "format": "inline", "params": { "date": { "label": "Month and year", "description": "The month and year that the template was placed (in full). \"May 2025\" inserts the current month and year automatically.", "type": "line", "autovalue": "May 2025", "example": "January 2013", "suggested": true }, "2": { "label": "Custom text", "description": "Text to replace \"a personal reflection, personal essay, or argumentative essay\" in the template", "type": "string", "example": "a forum post" }, "1": { "label": "Affected area", "description": "Text to replace the word \"article\", usually \"section\"", "type": "line", "autovalue": "section", "example": "section" }, "small": { "label": "Make template small", "description": "Enter \"left\" here to make the template box small and aligned to the left.", "type": "line", "autovalue": "left", "example": "left" } }, "paramOrder": [ "date", "2", "1", "small" ] } {{sandbox other|| [[Category:Tone cleanup templates]]}}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local/ILL link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Twinkle standard installation}} ==When to use== Use this tag to identify '''personal essays'''. Personal essays describe the author's own emotional [[:en:Feeling|Feeling]]s about a topic. Although Wikipedia is supposed to compile human knowledge, it is [[WP:What Wikipedia is not#PUBLISHER|not a vehicle to publish users' personal opinions]]. This template should be used when the article contains the editor's own personal, emotional comments on the subject. Use it when the article does not necessarily represent a blatant opinion or opinion piece, but is still overly judgmental in tone. ===When not to use=== '''Do not use this template to tag fact-oriented pages that sound like they might have been written as research papers for school''' (called "essays" in some parts of the world). Instead, if those pages need a different writing style or tone, use {{tl|Research paper}}, or general templates like {{tl|Tone}} or {{tl|Copy edit}}. If the article is biased, but without expressing the editor's emotions or value-based judgments, use tags like {{tl|unbalanced}} or {{tl|POV}}. ===How to use=== Place this: {{Template link with parameters|Essay-like|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} at the top of the article to alert editors to the inappropriate presence of editors' personal reflections throughout the article. ;Sections *To mark specific sections instead of the whole article, place {{tlx|Essay-like|section}} at the top of the section. ;Options *To replace the text "a personal reflection or essay", you may use {{tlx|Essay-like|article or section|yourtext}} or {{tlx|Essay-like|2=yourtext}} *Articles tagged without the optional date parameter will be given the parameter by a [[WP:Bots|bot]]. *Add a new item to the talk page explaining the problem so editors will know what to address, and when to remove this tag. ===Notes=== *This template will add tagged articles to dated subcategories of [[:Category:Wikipedia articles with style issues by month]] and to [[:Category:All articles with style issues]]. *This [[WP:Templates|template]] is a [[WP:Manual of Style/Self-references to avoid|self-reference]]. *Please do not [[WP:Substitution|subst:]] this template. ==See also== * [[:en:Template:Editorializing|Template:Editorializing]] – inline version of this template, for flagging particular statements * [[Template:Essay]] – identifies internal [[Wikipedia:Essays]] * [[:en:Template:How-to|Template:How-to]] * [[Template:Research paper]] * [[Wikipedia:Cleanup process]] * [[Wikipedia:Template messages/Cleanup]] ==TemplateData== {{TemplateData header}} { "description": "Use this cleanup template to indicate that an article is written like a personal reflection, personal essay, or argumentative essay that states a Wikipedia editor's personal feelings or presents an original argument about a topic.", "format": "inline", "params": { "date": { "label": "Month and year", "description": "The month and year that the template was placed (in full). \"May 2025\" inserts the current month and year automatically.", "type": "line", "autovalue": "May 2025", "example": "January 2013", "suggested": true }, "2": { "label": "Custom text", "description": "Text to replace \"a personal reflection, personal essay, or argumentative essay\" in the template", "type": "string", "example": "a forum post" }, "1": { "label": "Affected area", "description": "Text to replace the word \"article\", usually \"section\"", "type": "line", "autovalue": "section", "example": "section" }, "small": { "label": "Make template small", "description": "Enter \"left\" here to make the template box small and aligned to the left.", "type": "line", "autovalue": "left", "example": "left" } }, "paramOrder": [ "date", "2", "1", "small" ] } {{sandbox other|| [[Category:Tone cleanup templates]]}}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local/ILL link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Twinkle standard installation}} ==When to use== Use this tag to identify '''personal essays'''. Personal essays describe the author's own emotional [[:en:Feeling|Feeling]]s about a topic. Although Wikipedia is supposed to compile human knowledge, it is [[WP:What Wikipedia is not#PUBLISHER|not a vehicle to publish users' personal opinions]]. This template should be used when the article contains the editor's own personal, emotional comments on the subject. Use it when the article does not necessarily represent a blatant opinion or opinion piece, but is still overly judgmental in tone. ===When not to use=== '''Do not use this template to tag fact-oriented pages that sound like they might have been written as research papers for school''' (called "essays" in some parts of the world). Instead, if those pages need a different writing style or tone, use {{tl|Research paper}}, or general templates like {{tl|Tone}} or {{tl|Copy edit}}. If the article is biased, but without expressing the editor's emotions or value-based judgments, use tags like {{tl|unbalanced}} or {{tl|POV}}. ===How to use=== Place this: {{Template link with parameters|Essay-like|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} at the top of the article to alert editors to the inappropriate presence of editors' personal reflections throughout the article. ;Sections *To mark specific sections instead of the whole article, place {{tlx|Essay-like|section}} at the top of the section. ;Options *To replace the text "a personal reflection or essay", you may use {{tlx|Essay-like|article or section|yourtext}} or {{tlx|Essay-like|2=yourtext}} *Articles tagged without the optional date parameter will be given the parameter by a [[WP:Bots|bot]]. *Add a new item to the talk page explaining the problem so editors will know what to address, and when to remove this tag. ===Notes=== *This template will add tagged articles to dated subcategories of [[:Category:Wikipedia articles with style issues by month]] and to [[:Category:All articles with style issues]]. *This [[WP:Templates|template]] is a [[WP:Manual of Style/Self-references to avoid|self-reference]]. *Please do not [[WP:Substitution|subst:]] this template. ==See also== * [[:en:Template:Editorializing|Template:Editorializing]] – inline version of this template, for flagging particular statements * [[Template:Essay]] – identifies internal [[Wikipedia:Essays]] * [[:en:Template:How-to|Template:How-to]] * [[:en:Template:Research paper|Template:Research paper]] * [[Wikipedia:Cleanup process]] * [[Wikipedia:Template messages/Cleanup]] ==TemplateData== {{TemplateData header}} { "description": "Use this cleanup template to indicate that an article is written like a personal reflection, personal essay, or argumentative essay that states a Wikipedia editor's personal feelings or presents an original argument about a topic.", "format": "inline", "params": { "date": { "label": "Month and year", "description": "The month and year that the template was placed (in full). \"May 2025\" inserts the current month and year automatically.", "type": "line", "autovalue": "May 2025", "example": "January 2013", "suggested": true }, "2": { "label": "Custom text", "description": "Text to replace \"a personal reflection, personal essay, or argumentative essay\" in the template", "type": "string", "example": "a forum post" }, "1": { "label": "Affected area", "description": "Text to replace the word \"article\", usually \"section\"", "type": "line", "autovalue": "section", "example": "section" }, "small": { "label": "Make template small", "description": "Enter \"left\" here to make the template box small and aligned to the left.", "type": "line", "autovalue": "left", "example": "left" } }, "paramOrder": [ "date", "2", "1", "small" ] } {{sandbox other|| [[Category:Tone cleanup templates]]}}

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Twinkle standard installation}} ==When to use== Use this tag to identify '''personal essays'''. Personal essays describe the author's own emotional [[:en:Feeling|Feeling]]s about a topic. Although Wikipedia is supposed to compile human knowledge, it is [[WP:What Wikipedia is not#PUBLISHER|not a vehicle to publish users' personal opinions]]. This template should be used when the article contains the editor's own personal, emotional comments on the subject. Use it when the article does not necessarily represent a blatant opinion or opinion piece, but is still overly judgmental in tone. ===When not to use=== '''Do not use this template to tag fact-oriented pages that sound like they might have been written as research papers for school''' (called "essays" in some parts of the world). Instead, if those pages need a different writing style or tone, use {{tl|Research paper}}, or general templates like {{tl|Tone}} or {{tl|Copy edit}}. If the article is biased, but without expressing the editor's emotions or value-based judgments, use tags like {{tl|unbalanced}} or {{tl|POV}}. ===How to use=== Place this: {{Template link with parameters|Essay-like|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} at the top of the article to alert editors to the inappropriate presence of editors' personal reflections throughout the article. ;Sections *To mark specific sections instead of the whole article, place {{tlx|Essay-like|section}} at the top of the section. ;Options *To replace the text "a personal reflection or essay", you may use {{tlx|Essay-like|article or section|yourtext}} or {{tlx|Essay-like|2=yourtext}} *Articles tagged without the optional date parameter will be given the parameter by a [[WP:Bots|bot]]. *Add a new item to the talk page explaining the problem so editors will know what to address, and when to remove this tag. ===Notes=== *This template will add tagged articles to dated subcategories of [[:Category:Wikipedia articles with style issues by month]] and to [[:Category:All articles with style issues]]. *This [[WP:Templates|template]] is a [[WP:Manual of Style/Self-references to avoid|self-reference]]. *Please do not [[WP:Substitution|subst:]] this template. ==See also== * [[:en:Template:Editorializing|Template:Editorializing]] – inline version of this template, for flagging particular statements * [[Template:Essay]] – identifies internal [[Wikipedia:Essays]] * [[:en:Template:How-to|Template:How-to]] * [[:en:Template:Research paper|Template:Research paper]] * [[Wikipedia:Cleanup process]] * [[Wikipedia:Template messages/Cleanup]] ==TemplateData== {{TemplateData header}} { "description": "Use this cleanup template to indicate that an article is written like a personal reflection, personal essay, or argumentative essay that states a Wikipedia editor's personal feelings or presents an original argument about a topic.", "format": "inline", "params": { "date": { "label": "Month and year", "description": "The month and year that the template was placed (in full). \"May 2025\" inserts the current month and year automatically.", "type": "line", "autovalue": "May 2025", "example": "January 2013", "suggested": true }, "2": { "label": "Custom text", "description": "Text to replace \"a personal reflection, personal essay, or argumentative essay\" in the template", "type": "string", "example": "a forum post" }, "1": { "label": "Affected area", "description": "Text to replace the word \"article\", usually \"section\"", "type": "line", "autovalue": "section", "example": "section" }, "small": { "label": "Make template small", "description": "Enter \"left\" here to make the template box small and aligned to the left.", "type": "line", "autovalue": "left", "example": "left" } }, "paramOrder": [ "date", "2", "1", "small" ] } {{sandbox other|| [[Category:Tone cleanup templates]]}} [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Twinkle standard installation}} ==When to use== Use this tag to identify '''personal essays'''. Personal essays describe the author's own emotional [[:en:Feeling|Feeling]]s about a topic. Although Wikipedia is supposed to compile human knowledge, it is [[WP:What Wikipedia is not#PUBLISHER|not a vehicle to publish users' personal opinions]]. This template should be used when the article contains the editor's own personal, emotional comments on the subject. Use it when the article does not necessarily represent a blatant opinion or opinion piece, but is still overly judgmental in tone. ===When not to use=== '''Do not use this template to tag fact-oriented pages that sound like they might have been written as research papers for school''' (called "essays" in some parts of the world). Instead, if those pages need a different writing style or tone, use {{tl|Research paper}}, or general templates like {{tl|Tone}} or {{tl|Copy edit}}. If the article is biased, but without expressing the editor's emotions or value-based judgments, use tags like {{tl|unbalanced}} or {{tl|POV}}. ===How to use=== Place this: {{Template link with parameters|Essay-like|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} at the top of the article to alert editors to the inappropriate presence of editors' personal reflections throughout the article. ;Sections *To mark specific sections instead of the whole article, place {{tlx|Essay-like|section}} at the top of the section. ;Options *To replace the text "a personal reflection or essay", you may use {{tlx|Essay-like|article or section|yourtext}} or {{tlx|Essay-like|2=yourtext}} *Articles tagged without the optional date parameter will be given the parameter by a [[WP:Bots|bot]]. *Add a new item to the talk page explaining the problem so editors will know what to address, and when to remove this tag. ===Notes=== *This template will add tagged articles to dated subcategories of [[:Category:Wikipedia articles with style issues by month]] and to [[:Category:All articles with style issues]]. *This [[WP:Templates|template]] is a [[WP:Manual of Style/Self-references to avoid|self-reference]]. *Please do not [[WP:Substitution|subst:]] this template. ==See also== * [[:en:Template:Editorializing|Template:Editorializing]] – inline version of this template, for flagging particular statements * [[Template:Essay]] – identifies internal [[Wikipedia:Essays]] * [[:en:Template:How-to|Template:How-to]] * [[:en:Template:Research paper|Template:Research paper]] * [[Wikipedia:Cleanup process]] * [[Wikipedia:Template messages/Cleanup]] ==TemplateData== {{TemplateData header}} { "description": "Use this cleanup template to indicate that an article is written like a personal reflection, personal essay, or argumentative essay that states a Wikipedia editor's personal feelings or presents an original argument about a topic.", "format": "inline", "params": { "date": { "label": "Month and year", "description": "The month and year that the template was placed (in full). \"May 2025\" inserts the current month and year automatically.", "type": "line", "autovalue": "May 2025", "example": "January 2013", "suggested": true }, "2": { "label": "Custom text", "description": "Text to replace \"a personal reflection, personal essay, or argumentative essay\" in the template", "type": "string", "example": "a forum post" }, "1": { "label": "Affected area", "description": "Text to replace the word \"article\", usually \"section\"", "type": "line", "autovalue": "section", "example": "section" }, "small": { "label": "Make template small", "description": "Enter \"left\" here to make the template box small and aligned to the left.", "type": "line", "autovalue": "left", "example": "left" } }, "paramOrder": [ "date", "2", "1", "small" ] } {{sandbox other|| [[Category:Tone cleanup templates]]}}

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Twinkle standard installation}} ==When to use== Use this tag to identify '''personal essays'''. Personal essays describe the author's own emotional [[:en:Feeling|Feeling]]s about a topic. Although Wikipedia is supposed to compile human knowledge, it is [[WP:What Wikipedia is not#PUBLISHER|not a vehicle to publish users' personal opinions]]. This template should be used when the article contains the editor's own personal, emotional comments on the subject. Use it when the article does not necessarily represent a blatant opinion or opinion piece, but is still overly judgmental in tone. ===When not to use=== '''Do not use this template to tag fact-oriented pages that sound like they might have been written as research papers for school''' (called "essays" in some parts of the world). Instead, if those pages need a different writing style or tone, use {{tl|Research paper}}, or general templates like {{tl|Tone}} or {{tl|Copy edit}}. If the article is biased, but without expressing the editor's emotions or value-based judgments, use tags like {{tl|unbalanced}} or {{tl|POV}}. ===How to use=== Place this: {{Template link with parameters|Essay-like|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} at the top of the article to alert editors to the inappropriate presence of editors' personal reflections throughout the article. ;Sections *To mark specific sections instead of the whole article, place {{tlx|Essay-like|section}} at the top of the section. ;Options *To replace the text "a personal reflection or essay", you may use {{tlx|Essay-like|article or section|yourtext}} or {{tlx|Essay-like|2=yourtext}} *Articles tagged without the optional date parameter will be given the parameter by a [[WP:Bots|bot]]. *Add a new item to the talk page explaining the problem so editors will know what to address, and when to remove this tag. ===Notes=== *This template will add tagged articles to dated subcategories of [[:Category:Wikipedia articles with style issues by month]] and to [[:Category:All articles with style issues]]. *This [[WP:Templates|template]] is a [[WP:Manual of Style/Self-references to avoid|self-reference]]. *Please do not [[WP:Substitution|subst:]] this template. ==See also== * [[:en:Template:Editorializing|Template:Editorializing]] – inline version of this template, for flagging particular statements * [[Template:Essay]] – identifies internal [[Wikipedia:Essays]] * [[:en:Template:How-to|Template:How-to]] * [[:en:Template:Research paper|Template:Research paper]] * [[Wikipedia:Cleanup process]] * [[Wikipedia:Template messages/Cleanup]] ==TemplateData== {{TemplateData header}} { "description": "Use this cleanup template to indicate that an article is written like a personal reflection, personal essay, or argumentative essay that states a Wikipedia editor's personal feelings or presents an original argument about a topic.", "format": "inline", "params": { "date": { "label": "Month and year", "description": "The month and year that the template was placed (in full). \"May 2025\" inserts the current month and year automatically.", "type": "line", "autovalue": "May 2025", "example": "January 2013", "suggested": true }, "2": { "label": "Custom text", "description": "Text to replace \"a personal reflection, personal essay, or argumentative essay\" in the template", "type": "string", "example": "a forum post" }, "1": { "label": "Affected area", "description": "Text to replace the word \"article\", usually \"section\"", "type": "line", "autovalue": "section", "example": "section" }, "small": { "label": "Make template small", "description": "Enter \"left\" here to make the template box small and aligned to the left.", "type": "line", "autovalue": "left", "example": "left" } }, "paramOrder": [ "date", "2", "1", "small" ] } {{sandbox other|| [[Category:Tone cleanup templates]]}} [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 2 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23410164509|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Twinkle standard installation}} ==When to use== Use this tag to identify '''personal essays'''. Personal essays describe the author's own emotional [[:en:Feeling|Feeling]]s about a topic. Although Wikipedia is supposed to compile human knowledge, it is [[WP:What Wikipedia is not#PUBLISHER|not a vehicle to publish users' personal opinions]]. This template should be used when the article contains the editor's own personal, emotional comments on the subject. Use it when the article does not necessarily represent a blatant opinion or opinion piece, but is still overly judgmental in tone. ===When not to use=== '''Do not use this template to tag fact-oriented pages that sound like they might have been written as research papers for school''' (called "essays" in some parts of the world). Instead, if those pages need a different writing style or tone, use {{tl|Research paper}}, or general templates like {{tl|Tone}} or {{tl|Copy edit}}. If the article is biased, but without expressing the editor's emotions or value-based judgments, use tags like {{tl|unbalanced}} or {{tl|POV}}. ===How to use=== Place this: {{Template link with parameters|Essay-like|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} at the top of the article to alert editors to the inappropriate presence of editors' personal reflections throughout the article. ;Sections *To mark specific sections instead of the whole article, place {{tlx|Essay-like|section}} at the top of the section. ;Options *To replace the text "a personal reflection or essay", you may use {{tlx|Essay-like|article or section|yourtext}} or {{tlx|Essay-like|2=yourtext}} *Articles tagged without the optional date parameter will be given the parameter by a [[WP:Bots|bot]]. *Add a new item to the talk page explaining the problem so editors will know what to address, and when to remove this tag. ===Notes=== *This template will add tagged articles to dated subcategories of [[:Category:Wikipedia articles with style issues by month]] and to [[:Category:All articles with style issues]]. *This [[WP:Templates|template]] is a [[WP:Manual of Style/Self-references to avoid|self-reference]]. *Please do not [[WP:Substitution|subst:]] this template. ==See also== * [[:en:Template:Editorializing|Template:Editorializing]] – inline version of this template, for flagging particular statements * [[Template:Essay]] – identifies internal [[Wikipedia:Essays]] * [[:en:Template:How-to|Template:How-to]] * [[:en:Template:Research paper|Template:Research paper]] * [[Wikipedia:Cleanup process]] * [[Wikipedia:Template messages/Cleanup]] ==TemplateData== {{TemplateData header}} { "description": "Use this cleanup template to indicate that an article is written like a personal reflection, personal essay, or argumentative essay that states a Wikipedia editor's personal feelings or presents an original argument about a topic.", "format": "inline", "params": { "date": { "label": "Month and year", "description": "The month and year that the template was placed (in full). \"May 2025\" inserts the current month and year automatically.", "type": "line", "autovalue": "May 2025", "example": "January 2013", "suggested": true }, "2": { "label": "Custom text", "description": "Text to replace \"a personal reflection, personal essay, or argumentative essay\" in the template", "type": "string", "example": "a forum post" }, "1": { "label": "Affected area", "description": "Text to replace the word \"article\", usually \"section\"", "type": "line", "autovalue": "section", "example": "section" }, "small": { "label": "Make template small", "description": "Enter \"left\" here to make the template box small and aligned to the left.", "type": "line", "autovalue": "left", "example": "left" } }, "paramOrder": [ "date", "2", "1", "small" ] } {{sandbox other|| }} [[Category:Tone cleanup templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

revision.comment: [[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] many contributors, see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Essay-like%2Fdoc here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Twinkle standard installation}} ==When to use== Use this tag to identify '''personal essays'''. Personal essays describe the author's own emotional [[:en:Feeling|Feeling]]s about a topic. Although Wikipedia is supposed to compile human knowledge, it is [[WP:What Wikipedia is not#PUBLISHER|not a vehicle to publish users' personal opinions]]. This template should be used when the article contains the editor's own personal, emotional comments on the subject. Use it when the article does not necessarily represent a blatant opinion or opinion piece, but is still overly judgmental in tone. ===When not to use=== '''Do not use this template to tag fact-oriented pages that sound like they might have been written as research papers for school''' (called "essays" in some parts of the world). Instead, if those pages need a different writing style or tone, use {{tl|Research paper}}, or general templates like {{tl|Tone}} or {{tl|Copy edit}}. If the article is biased, but without expressing the editor's emotions or value-based judgments, use tags like {{tl|unbalanced}} or {{tl|POV}}. ===How to use=== Place this: {{Template link with parameters|Essay-like|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} at the top of the article to alert editors to the inappropriate presence of editors' personal reflections throughout the article. ;Sections *To mark specific sections instead of the whole article, place {{tlx|Essay-like|section}} at the top of the section. ;Options *To replace the text "a personal reflection or essay", you may use {{tlx|Essay-like|article or section|yourtext}} or {{tlx|Essay-like|2=yourtext}} *Articles tagged without the optional date parameter will be given the parameter by a [[WP:Bots|bot]]. *Add a new item to the talk page explaining the problem so editors will know what to address, and when to remove this tag. ===Notes=== *This template will add tagged articles to dated subcategories of [[:Category:Wikipedia articles with style issues by month]] and to [[:Category:All articles with style issues]]. *This [[WP:Templates|template]] is a [[WP:Manual of Style/Self-references to avoid|self-reference]]. *Please do not [[WP:Substitution|subst:]] this template. ==See also== * [[:en:Template:Editorializing|Template:Editorializing]] – inline version of this template, for flagging particular statements * [[Template:Essay]] – identifies internal [[Wikipedia:Essays]] * [[:en:Template:How-to|Template:How-to]] * [[:en:Template:Research paper|Template:Research paper]] * [[Wikipedia:Cleanup process]] * [[Wikipedia:Template messages/Cleanup]] ==TemplateData== {{TemplateData header}} { "description": "Use this cleanup template to indicate that an article is written like a personal reflection, personal essay, or argumentative essay that states a Wikipedia editor's personal feelings or presents an original argument about a topic.", "format": "inline", "params": { "date": { "label": "Month and year", "description": "The month and year that the template was placed (in full). \"May 2025\" inserts the current month and year automatically.", "type": "line", "autovalue": "May 2025", "example": "January 2013", "suggested": true }, "2": { "label": "Custom text", "description": "Text to replace \"a personal reflection, personal essay, or argumentative essay\" in the template", "type": "string", "example": "a forum post" }, "1": { "label": "Affected area", "description": "Text to replace the word \"article\", usually \"section\"", "type": "line", "autovalue": "section", "example": "section" }, "small": { "label": "Make template small", "description": "Enter \"left\" here to make the template box small and aligned to the left.", "type": "line", "autovalue": "left", "example": "left" } }, "paramOrder": [ "date", "2", "1", "small" ] } {{sandbox other|| }} [[Category:Tone cleanup templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

revision.comment: Bot: strip HTML comments [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24931715792|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Twinkle standard installation}} ==When to use== Use this tag to identify '''personal essays'''. Personal essays describe the author's own emotional [[:en:Feeling|Feeling]]s about a topic. Although Wikipedia is supposed to compile human knowledge, it is [[WP:What Wikipedia is not#PUBLISHER|not a vehicle to publish users' personal opinions]]. This template should be used when the article contains the editor's own personal, emotional comments on the subject. Use it when the article does not necessarily represent a blatant opinion or opinion piece, but is still overly judgmental in tone. ===When not to use=== '''Do not use this template to tag fact-oriented pages that sound like they might have been written as research papers for school''' (called "essays" in some parts of the world). Instead, if those pages need a different writing style or tone, use {{tl|Research paper}}, or general templates like {{tl|Tone}} or {{tl|Copy edit}}. If the article is biased, but without expressing the editor's emotions or value-based judgments, use tags like {{tl|unbalanced}} or {{tl|POV}}. ===How to use=== Place this: {{Template link with parameters|Essay-like|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} at the top of the article to alert editors to the inappropriate presence of editors' personal reflections throughout the article. ;Sections *To mark specific sections instead of the whole article, place {{tlx|Essay-like|section}} at the top of the section. ;Options *To replace the text "a personal reflection or essay", you may use {{tlx|Essay-like|article or section|yourtext}} or {{tlx|Essay-like|2=yourtext}} *Articles tagged without the optional date parameter will be given the parameter by a [[WP:Bots|bot]]. *Add a new item to the talk page explaining the problem so editors will know what to address, and when to remove this tag. ===Notes=== *This template will add tagged articles to dated subcategories of [[:Category:Wikipedia articles with style issues by month]] and to [[:Category:All articles with style issues]]. *This [[WP:Templates|template]] is a [[WP:Manual of Style/Self-references to avoid|self-reference]]. *Please do not [[WP:Substitution|subst:]] this template. ==See also== * [[:en:Template:Editorializing|Template:Editorializing]] – inline version of this template, for flagging particular statements * [[Template:Essay]] – identifies internal [[Wikipedia:Essays]] * [[:en:Template:How-to|Template:How-to]] * [[:en:Template:Research paper|Template:Research paper]] * [[Wikipedia:Cleanup process]] * [[Wikipedia:Template messages/Cleanup]] ==TemplateData== {{TemplateData header}} { "description": "Use this cleanup template to indicate that an article is written like a personal reflection, personal essay, or argumentative essay that states a Wikipedia editor's personal feelings or presents an original argument about a topic.", "format": "inline", "params": { "date": { "label": "Month and year", "description": "The month and year that the template was placed (in full). \"May 2025\" inserts the current month and year automatically.", "type": "line", "autovalue": "May 2025", "example": "January 2013", "suggested": true }, "2": { "label": "Custom text", "description": "Text to replace \"a personal reflection, personal essay, or argumentative essay\" in the template", "type": "string", "example": "a forum post" }, "1": { "label": "Affected area", "description": "Text to replace the word \"article\", usually \"section\"", "type": "line", "autovalue": "section", "example": "section" }, "small": { "label": "Make template small", "description": "Enter \"left\" here to make the template box small and aligned to the left.", "type": "line", "autovalue": "left", "example": "left" } }, "paramOrder": [ "date", "2", "1", "small" ] } {{sandbox other|| }} [[Category:Tone cleanup templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
